name: girepository-tools
title: girepository-tools
summary: Tools for working with GObject-Introspection repositories
description:
Tools for working with GObject-Introspection repositories
GLib is a library containing many useful C routines for things such
as trees, hashes, lists, and strings.  It is a useful general-purpose
C library used by projects such as GTK, GIMP, and GNOME.
.
This package contains developer-oriented command-line tools for working
with GObject-Introspection typelibs and GIR XML, which are used to
provide language bindings and documentation for GLib-based libraries:
.
* x86_64-linux-gnu-gi-compile-repository
* x86_64-linux-gnu-gi-decompile-typelib
* x86_64-linux-gnu-gi-inspect-typelib
.
When used via this package, these tools must be invoked with their
host-architecture-specific names.
.
For command-line compatibility with non-Debian-based GLib packaging,
the libglib2.0-dev package also provides versions of these tools for the
system's primary architecture, named without the architecture prefix.

arguments.description: Add DIRECTORY to the search path for GIR XML. This option can be used more than once.
arguments.raw: --includedir  Add DIRECTORY to the search path for GIR XML. This option can be used more than once.

arguments.description: abihf-gi-compile-repository. These architecture-prefixed names use appropriate search paths
arguments.raw: -linux-gnue  abihf-gi-compile-repository. These architecture-prefixed names use appropriate search paths

arguments.description: Show program's version number and exit.
arguments.raw: --version  Show program's version number and exit.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use x86_64-linux-gnu-gi-compile-repository because Tools for working with GObject-Introspection repositories.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use x86_64-linux-gnu-gi-compile-repository as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair x86_64-linux-gnu-gi-compile-repository with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `x86_64-linux-gnu-gi-compile-repository --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: --includedir
arguments.description: Add DIRECTORY to the search path for GIR XML. This option can be used more than once.

arguments.name: -linux-gnue
arguments.description: abihf-gi-compile-repository. These architecture-prefixed names use appropriate search paths

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Tools for working with GObject-Introspection repositories.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Tools for working with GObject-Introspection repositories.

Install with: sudo apt install glib2.0
